mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust labor market and healthy economic expansion, which typically translates into strong corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above a critical threshold signals potential central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations, leading to lower S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:inflationary pressures often lead to rate hikes
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns remain positive or flat in more than 40% of periods where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 4.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies strong consumer confidence and spending power, directly boosting corporate revenues and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major component of GDP
failure_condition: When RSAFS growth is positive and UNRATE is below 6%, SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of exceptionally low federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS), stable or slightly increasing industrial production (INDPRO) indicates economic recovery without overheating, supporting positive equity returns as capital costs remain low.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.5% and INDPRO is growing, SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals strong investment and consumer confidence in the housing sector, which typically precedes broader economic growth and positive equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:housing is a cyclical leading indicator
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods following a 10% 3-month average growth in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 30% of periods where UNRATE has risen by 0.5pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects robust manufacturing and supply chain activity, indicating healthy corporate output and contributing to positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where INDPRO 3-month average growth exceeds 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation (CPIAUCSL) coupled with slowing GDP growth (GDPC1) creates a stagflationary environment, pressuring corporate margins and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as monetary policy faces a difficult trade-off.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month change > 3% AND GDPC1 1-quarter annualized growth < 2%
evidence_refs: fact:stagflation is detrimental to equity markets
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of periods where CPIAUCSL 12-month change > 3% and GDPC1 1-quarter annualized growth < 2%.
